mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5.0% and remains elevated for 2+ consecutive months, the restrictive monetary stance increases the cost of capital for equities, depressing S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days. Higher FEDFUNDS directly compresses equity valuations through higher discount rates.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with restrictive rates above neutral
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite FEDFUNDS > 5.0%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window during tightening regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month payroll employment growth (PAYEMS) serves as a real-time signal of labor market momentum. Sustained payroll growth above 150,000 persons per month indicates robust demand and earnings growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as investors price in stronger corporate profits.
failure_condition: Rank IC between PAYEMS growth and 60-day forward returns falls below 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive payroll months do not produce significantly higher forward returns than negative payroll months (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Real retail sales (RSAFS) declining month-over-month signals weakening consumer demand and future earnings pressure on corporations. A 2%+ month-over-month contraction in RSAFS predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as markets reprrice growth expectations downward.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of instances following RSAFS declines > 2%, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) and the unemployment rate (UNRATE) jointly signal supply-side capacity utilization and labor slack. When INDPRO growth accelerates while UNRATE remains below 4.0%, the economy operates near full capacity with tight labor markets, increasing inflation risk and prompting equity market repricing downward over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 102.67 and UNRATE at 3.7% as of 2023-11-01 and 2023-12-01
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor markets and supply constraints
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 70% of triggered instances despite tight supply and labor conditions, or the interaction coefficient between INDPRO and UNRATE is not statistically significant (p > 0.05) in logistic regression on forward return direction.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) reflect forward-looking demand expectations and construction sector health. A sustained 3-month decline in HOUST below 1,400 thousand units signals weakening demand momentum and reduced future economic activity, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as investors reprrice growth and earnings.
candidate_trigger: HOUST < 1,400 thousand units for 3+ consecutive months
regime_hint: demand-constrained environment with rising rates or credit tightening
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following HOUST < 1,400 are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.10), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPIAUCSL and the federal funds rate jointly determine real monetary conditions. When CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth exceeds 4.0% while FEDFUNDS remains below 4.5%, monetary policy is accommodative in real terms, supporting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 308.85 and FEDFUNDS at 5.33% as of 2023-12-01
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with persistent inflation but accommodative real rates
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite accommodative real rates, or the interaction term between CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS is not statistically significant (p > 0.05) in predicting forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate (UNRATE) rising by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month period signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending power. This triggers negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as markets anticipate earnings pressure from lower consumer demand.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recession onset
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of instances following UNRATE increases > 0.3pp, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) and retail sales (RSAFS) jointly capture aggregate demand momentum. When GDPC1 annualized growth exceeds 2.5% and RSAFS shows month-over-month growth > 0.3%, the economy is in sustained expansion, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days through higher corporate earnings and profit margins.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth > 2.5% AND RSAFS month-over-month growth > 0.3%
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 22,490.69 billion and RSAFS at 705,692 million as of 2023-07-01 and 2023-11-01
regime_hint: sustained economic expansion with strong demand growth
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 45% of triggered instances despite strong GDP and retail sales growth, or the interaction coefficient between GDPC1 and RSAFS is not statistically significant (p > 0.05) in logistic regression on forward return direction.
